qa.user_verbatim_complaint: When a user uses /allow <command>, agents must be able to execute that command — including commands that currently hit the ref-mutation hard wall (e.g. git branch -D, git worktree remove --force). No exceptions. User consent is sovereign.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() is now called in _check_git_allowlist() at lines 156-158, before the subagent firewall check. All 8 pytest tests (AC1-AC8) pass, confirming that a user-granted sentinel allows ref-mutation commands for both main-agent and subagent callers.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: function name + file path from BA spec

qa.overall_assessment: Implementation matches BA spec exactly. All 8 ACs pass in pytest (8 passed in 0.03s). No TEST_INCOMPLETE stubs. Force-push security ordering verified: _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation() calls _block(sys.exit(2)) at line 617, hard-blocking before _check_git_allowlist() at line 624 is ever reached — a sentinel grant cannot bypass force-push. One spec_text_vs_execution_drift objection raised on AC8 and one on AC1-AC7 file path drift. Both are BA-spec corrections required but do not block the cycle's implementation correctness.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: When a user uses /allow <command>, agents must be able to execute that command
success_criteria_results.criterion: GIVEN a valid sentinel grant for git branch -D foo, WHEN an agent runs git branch -D foo, THEN privilege guard allows it (exit 0)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: pytest test_sentinel_grant_bypasses_guard_main_agent (AC1): writes sentinel grant, calls _check_git_allowlist('git branch -D foo', {}), asserts True
success_criteria_results.details: Test passes. Implementation at line 156-158 in _check_git_allowlist() calls match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command(task_id, command) before subagent firewall. Returns True when sentinel matches.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: including commands that currently hit the ref-mutation hard wall (e.g. git branch -D)
success_criteria_results.criterion: GIVEN a sentinel for git branch -D foo, WHEN _check_git_allowlist() is called, THEN match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() is consulted and returns a match
success_criteria_results.verification_method: pytest test_sentinel_grant_bypasses_guard_main_agent (AC1) and test_sentinel_does_not_match_unrelated_command (AC6): directly verify sentinel match logic
success_criteria_results.details: match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() is imported at line 92 and called at line 157. Returns the matched entry dict (not None) for matching commands, None for non-matching. Confirmed by reading lib/allowlist.py:438-481.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest: AC1 PASSED, AC6 PASSED (sentinel for 'branch -D foo' does not match 'git status')

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: No exceptions. User consent is sovereign.
success_criteria_results.criterion: GIVEN a sentinel created by /allow, WHEN any agent (subagent or main-agent) runs the allowed command, THEN the subagent firewall does NOT suppress the sentinel check
success_criteria_results.verification_method: pytest test_sentinel_grant_bypasses_subagent_firewall (AC2): calls _check_git_allowlist with agent_id set, asserts True
success_criteria_results.details: Sentinel check at line 156-158 precedes the subagent firewall at line 160. With agent_id present, the sentinel path still returns True if a match is found. Legacy path firewall at line 160 only applies after the sentinel check returns False.

qa._success_criteria_rule: All 3 requirements from requirements_decomposition verified with passing evidence.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause was the absence of match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() in _check_git_allowlist(). The fix adds it at line 156-158 before the subagent firewall. The git diff at commit 86608dc confirms the exact change: import added at line 92, 5 lines replaced with 12 lines in _check_git_allowlist(). The two parallel PreToolUse hooks (bash-safety.sh and privilege-guard.py) now both honor sentinel grants consistently.

build_verification.skip_reason: Non-UI hook change. Hook is a Python script executed directly by the Claude harness — no build or deploy step. Import-level syntax verified implicitly by pytest loading the module via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location().

regression_test_results.test: No sentinel, no legacy grant — default deny preserved (AC3)
regression_test_results.details: test_no_grant_returns_false: _check_git_allowlist returns False when no grant files exist. Default-deny behavior unchanged.

regression_test_results.test: Legacy grant still works for main-agent (AC4)
regression_test_results.details: test_legacy_grant_still_works_main_agent: legacy /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json grant with pattern match returns True for main-agent. Legacy path preserved.

regression_test_results.test: Subagent firewall preserved for legacy grant path (AC5)
regression_test_results.details: test_legacy_grant_subagent_firewall_preserved: with legacy grant only (no sentinel), agent_id set, returns False. Subagent firewall on legacy path intact.

regression_test_results.test: Sentinel does not over-match unrelated commands (AC6)
regression_test_results.details: test_sentinel_does_not_match_unrelated_command: sentinel with args_contain=['branch -D foo'] does not match 'git status'. Confirms structural match is scoped — args_contain check on 'rest' (tokens after head_op) catches this correctly.
test_expired_sentinel_not_honored: sentinel with expires_at = time.time()-1 returns None from load_sentinel_grant_for_task(). _check_git_allowlist returns False.

test_push_ordering_preserved: AST walk of _evaluate_push() confirms min(forbidden_lines)=617 < min(allowlist_lines)=624. Force-push block at line 617 calls _block(sys.exit(2)) and never returns — sentinel cannot bypass force-push regardless of ordering.

regression_test_results.test: Provenance check: git diff confirms change is in working tree
regression_test_results.details: git show 86608dc confirms diff at hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py matching dev report description. Files_modified=['hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py'] consistent with committed change.

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: Not applicable — non-UI hook change. Setup.applicability: N/A per context JSON. No deployed service to health-check.

generated_tests.description: 8 tests covering AC1-AC8: sentinel bypass main-agent, sentinel bypass subagent, no-grant default-deny, legacy grant main-agent, legacy subagent firewall, sentinel no-overmatch, expired sentinel, _evaluate_push ordering

manifest_verification._note: No per-task manifest.json exists. test_writer was not run upstream for this task (BA did not emit a per-task manifest). Tests were written by dev agent directly. pytest collected and passed 8 tests from tests/generated/20260524-133650/ without manifest.

blast_radius_phase2._note: blast-radius-tool.py not run directly; git diff --name-only at HEAD confirms hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is the sole modified file, matching dev.files_modified. dev.blast_radius_declarations covers the one modified file.

all_findings.issue: spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC1-AC7 check.file points to 'hooks/tests/test_allowlist_sentinel_privilege_guard.py' which does not exist. Actual test suite is at 'tests/generated/20260524-133650/test_allowlist_sentinel_privilege_guard.py'. A close verifier following the AC file literally will not find the tests.
all_findings.recommendation: BA must update acceptance-criteria-20260524-133650.json AC1-AC7 check.file to 'tests/generated/20260524-133650/test_allowlist_sentinel_privilege_guard.py'.

all_findings.issue: spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC8 check.command is a Python one-liner that only prints 'OK' when _evaluate_push is found — it does not assert the ordering. The assert field says 'Manual grep'. The actual pytest test uses AST walking with assertLess(min(forbidden_lines), min(allowlist_lines)) which properly verifies the ordering invariant. The AC literal text's command field is non-verifying.
all_findings.recommendation: BA must update AC8 check.command to the AST-based ordering assertion actually used in the test, or mark it as 'type: pytest' pointing to test_push_ordering_preserved.

all_findings.issue: Test cleanup does not remove /tmp/claude-grants/<TEST_SID>.json. AC4/AC5 tests strip CLAUDE_TASK_ID from env, so _check_git_allowlist() falls back to sid as task_id and probes /tmp/claude-grants/test-session-20260524-133650.json. A stale sentinel at that path from a prior test run could cause AC4/AC5 to pass for the wrong reason.
all_findings.recommendation: Add _remove_sentinel(TEST_SID) to setUp() and tearDown() to ensure both task_id-keyed and SID-keyed sentinel files are cleaned up.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding 1 (out of scope): real /allow git branch -D foo writes allowed_operations: [{op:'git'}] without args_contain, because the writer splits the pattern on first whitespace and emits the remainder as args_contain. But the AC tests manually craft a sentinel with args_contain=['branch -D foo']. This means real /allow invocations write a broader grant than the AC tests exercise. Separate ticket already logged in dev report recommendations.

Codex finding 1-variant: if _get_session_id(data) returns empty string (data={} with no session_id), the function returns False before the sentinel check even when CLAUDE_TASK_ID is set. The BA spec's proposed 'After' code has the same guard. All AC tests pass session_id in data. This is an edge case the BA spec implicitly accepted — the PreToolUse data payload always contains session_id in practice. Not a regression introduced by this cycle; the 'if not sid: return False' guard was present before this cycle and the BA spec preserved it. Out of scope for this cycle.

Codex finding 5 (observation): force-push is blocked unconditionally before _check_git_allowlist is reached. The BA spec explicitly requires this ordering and notes it MUST NOT be altered. User requirement says 'No exceptions' broadly, but BA spec Edge Case #5 explicitly constrains: force-push ordering is intentional and out of scope for this ticket.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 4 findings + 1 observation. (1) MAJOR: sentinel skipped when session_id absent — evaluated as out_of_scope: BA spec proposed code has same guard, AC tests always pass session_id, not a regression. (2) MAJOR: tests call _check_git_allowlist() directly rather than full hook subprocess — evaluated as out_of_scope: BA spec prescribes direct function call testing. (3) MAJOR: AC1-AC7 file path drift (hooks/tests/ vs tests/generated/) — incorporated as spec_text_vs_execution_drift minor finding. (4) MINOR: tearDown does not remove TEST_SID sentinel — incorporated as minor dev finding. (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY: force-push exception — logged as out_of_scope_observation.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 3 and 4 incorporated as minor findings. Findings 1, 2, 5 classified as out_of_scope per BA spec constraints and won't-have list. No verdict change — all are minor/observation-only and do not block the cycle's core requirement.
